nodes.name: Send Email: S5.2 Spouse Uncertainty D4 Email

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "A read for both of you, {{contact.first_name}}", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
The conversation most couples skip — and what happens next. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

I want to share something I've watched play out hundreds of times in {{contact.city}} homes.

A couple gets a hurricane window quote. One partner is sold — saw the storm damage two doors down, gets it. The other partner needs more time. The first partner pushes. The second partner digs in. The conversation goes sideways. Six months later, they're still on the original windows, still arguing, and they've added a roof leak to the list.

Here's what I've learned: this isn't a disagreement problem. It's an information problem.

When both decision-makers have the same picture — what's actually behind your current windows, what the warranty really covers, what financing looks like — the conversation gets a lot easier.

So I'd like to suggest something different. Instead of rebooking the estimate right away, here's a short read for both of you:

→ The 3-minute homeowner's brief

It covers what a real impact window assessment looks like, what we found in the last 50 South Florida homes we visited, and the warranty that survives the next owner.

If after reading it you both want to sit down with a rep — together — I'll hold a 90-minute slot for whatever evening works.

Grab a Slot

Or reply to this email with a time, and I'll hold it.

— Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors

P.S. The brief is short on purpose. If your partner only reads the first paragraph, that's the right paragraph.
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.address}}, {{location.city}}, {{location.state}} {{location.postal_code}}
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Send Email: S5.2 - Trust Hesitation D4 Email (Randy)

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "Why I'm careful about who I let into your home", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
A short story about why Reece exists — and what we won't do. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

{{contact.first_name}},

Quick story.

My father Michael, started Reece in North Carolina in 1972. He was an installer first. He'd seen what happened when someone got sold cheap windows by a company that disappeared two years later — the homeowner called the manufacturer, got transferred three times, then quoted $4,800 for a part that should have been covered.

My father moved the family business to Florida in 2005, right after Wilma. He saw what hurricane impact windows did for homes that had them. He also saw what fly-by-night contractors did to homeowners who didn't know which questions to ask.

So Reece does a few things differently. I want you to know them before you book anything:

1. Our warranty transfers. Sell the house in 8 years? The new owner is covered too — parts AND labor — for the lifetime of the windows. Most companies cover parts only, and only for the original owner.

2. One rep, one estimate, no rotation. You'll meet one person, that person will walk your home with you, and that person is who shows up on install day. We don't have a rotating sales team.

3. We don't pitch on the first visit if the home doesn't need it. If your current windows are 4 years old and properly rated, we'll tell you. We've left without a deposit more times than I can count.

4. We hold the install license. We're not subcontracting your install to someone you've never met.

If any of that matters to you, the next step is a 90-minute walkthrough of your home with one of our reps. No deposit, no pressure, no "today only" pricing. You'll get a written assessment and an honest answer about whether new windows make sense for your situation.

Book a Slot

Or read the warranty itself — it's two pages, no fine print: The Reece Warranty

— Talk soon,

{{custom_values.founder_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors

P.S. If you've already gotten quotes from other companies, bring them. We'll tell you what's apples-to-apples and what's not. That's the kind of thing Randy would do.
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.address}}, {{location.city}}, {{location.state}} {{location.postal_code}}
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Send Email: S5.2 Price Anxiety D3 Email

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "A ballpark before the appointment, {{contact.first_name}}", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
Three-minute calculator. Real numbers. No login. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

{{contact.first_name}},

Most quotes you'll get for impact windows in {{contact.city}} have a 35–60% spread. That's not a typo. The same home, the same number of openings, can get quotes ranging from $18,000 to $38,000 depending on who's pitching.

That spread isn't because contractors are dishonest. It's because most "estimates" aren't estimates — they're starting points for negotiation. And nobody tells you the real number until you've sat through a 2-hour sales pitch.

That's a bad deal for you. So we built something different.

→ The Reece Estimate Calculator

It takes about 3 minutes. You'll enter:

• Number of openings (windows + doors)

• Approximate square footage of your home

• ZIP code (for permit + install costs in your area)

• Window style preference (basic vs upgraded glass)

You'll get back a real range — not a teaser, not a "book a consultation" wall. Just the number.

If that number works for your budget, the next step is the in-home walkthrough. If it doesn't, you've saved yourself two hours.

Either way, you walk out with information instead of a sales pitch.

Run the Calculator

Or if you'd rather skip ahead and book the in-home assessment: grab a slot here.

— Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors

P.S. We built the calculator after watching too many homeowners book appointments, sit through quotes, and then find out the project was double their budget. Information up front is the only fair way.
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.address}}, {{location.city}}, {{location.state}} {{location.postal_code}}
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Assign User: Assign to Randy Reece

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "We got the order backwards", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
No rebook ask in this email. Just an honest reframe of what should have been said first. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

{{contact.first_name}},

Randy Reece, founder of Reece Windows & Doors. {{custom_values.rep_name}} from our Homeowner Review Team let me know your appointment was cancelled, and I asked him to wait on rebooking anything until I sent this.

I’m not writing to ask you to reschedule. I’m writing because most homeowners who cancel a meeting with us didn’t actually do anything wrong — we just skipped a step.

Here’s what the meeting was supposed to be about.

Most homeowners assume they know what their home was engineered to handle. There’s one number that helps confirm it. It can quietly affect four things at once: storm protection, insurance treatment, whether your windows match Florida code requirements for your zone, and resale value. It’s called the impact rating.

And honestly, here’s the part that bothers me: what’s installed and what’s on record don’t always match. Same window, same house, two different answers depending on where you look. That mismatch usually doesn’t show up at install. It shows up later.

A lot of homeowners check and everything lines up just fine. That’s actually the common outcome. But knowing for sure is the part that matters, because the homeowners who get surprised later are usually the ones who never looked.

The in-home appointment you scheduled was, at its core, our team coming out to confirm what your home is actually rated for, and whether it matches your zone — your protection profile, made real instead of assumed.

That’s it.

If you’d known that going in, the meeting might have made more sense, or you might have decided you didn’t need it. Either way, you would have decided with the full picture, not before you had it.

Tomorrow {{custom_values.rep_name}} will send where the mismatch usually shows up. After that, if you want to know your number, there’s a 15-min Protection Profile Review you can book — no in-home, no quote.

{{custom_values.founder_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors
P.S. If you’d rather just do the Review now: book your Protection Profile Review.
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.address}}, {{location.city}}, {{location.state}} {{location.postal_code}}
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Assign User: Assign to Mark from Reece
Send Email: S5.2 1 Leg D4 Email

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "Round 2, {{contact.first_name}} - when you're both home", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
What we cover, why it matters both of you see it. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

{{contact.first_name}},

Quick follow-up after our visit.

I want to be straight with you about why we don't run the full assessment with just one partner present — it's not a policy, it's experience.

What we cover in the walkthrough:

• What's behind your current windows (rot, gaps, frame separation — usually invisible from inside)

• What your specific glass package would actually do during a Cat-3 vs Cat-5 event

• The warranty paperwork — page by page, no fine print

• Financing options, if applicable

• A real number, in writing, with the install timeline

That's about 90 minutes. We've learned that when one partner sees all that and the other doesn't, the second partner ends up making a decision on a summary — not the actual information. And summaries get distorted. The conversation goes from "here's what we saw" to "you don't trust me to handle this" and we've just made your week harder.

So the ask is simple: a 90-minute slot when you're both home. Evenings work. Weekends work.

Grab a Time

Or reply to this email and tell me what evenings work — I'll hold a slot.

— Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors

P.S. If your partner has specific questions they want answered before they sit through the assessment, send them over. I'll answer them in writing and you can both review before we meet. Some couples find that helps.
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.address}}, {{location.city}}, {{location.state}} {{location.postal_code}}
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Assign User: Assign to Mark from Reece #2
Send Email: S5.2 Be Back D4 Email

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "Picking up where we left off, {{contact.first_name}}", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
What we covered, what's left, and the next 60 minutes. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

{{contact.first_name}},

Following up on our visit. We didn't get to finish, and I want to make picking it up easy.

Here's what we already covered (so we don't repeat):

• Walked your openings

• Looked at the current window condition

• Discussed your hurricane coverage concerns

Here's what's left:

• The specific glass package recommendation for your home

• The written estimate (with the transferable warranty terms)

• Financing options if you want them

• Install timeline and what to expect on install day

That's about 60 minutes — faster than starting over.

Grab a Slot to Finish

If anything came up after our visit — a question, a concern, second thoughts — reply to this email and tell me. Easier to talk about it now than to drag it out for weeks.

— Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors

P.S. If you've decided this isn't the right time, that's a real answer too. Let me know either way so I can free up the slot for someone else.
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.address}}, {{location.city}}, {{location.state}} {{location.postal_code}}
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

parameters.content:
## S5.2 Appointment Rescue (Policy Executor) v2
Translated from GHL workflow 0a6a1349-0b44-429b-91e1-4c5be264cd9f.
GHL triggers: inbound_webhook:Inbound Agentic Trigger

Entry is a webhook (orchestrator-driven). Credentials by ID only; brand law applied to email senders (Randy Reece). See workflow node notes for [STUB] items.
Flags: 26 | Stubs: 1 | Brand-law: 6
